description: Qt 6.8+ Integration and Platform-specific Drivers - Update build system for conditional Qt6 support and create platform abstraction layer
steps: Update src/app/CMakeLists.txt to conditionally add Qt6 targets when ENABLE_QT=ON: link Qt6::Core, Qt6::Quick, Qt6::Widgets, add qt_add_qml_module for QML files from qml/ directory, register all ViewModels as QML types. When ENABLE_QT=OFF, keep current build unchanged.

Update src/app/src/main.cpp: Fill in the #ifdef ENABLE_QT_BUILD block with proper QGuiApplication, QQmlApplicationEngine, QML type registration (qmlRegisterType for all 10 ViewModels), engine.load for qrc:/qml/main.qml. Keep the non-Qt console loop as-is.
Create cmake/Platform.cmake to detect OS (Linux, Windows, macOS) and set platform-specific variables. Add option ENABLE_PLATFORM_DRIVERS (default OFF).

Create src/platform/ module with CMakeLists.txt, include/platform/IPlatformDriver.h (abstract interface with virtual methods for file monitoring, network filtering, process monitoring), include/platform/PlatformFactory.h (factory to create platform-appropriate driver).

Create src/platform/src/LinuxDriver.h/.cpp: real inotify-based file monitoring (inotify_init, inotify_add_watch, read events), netfilter interface (iptables rule management via system() calls to /sbin/iptables), /proc-based process enumeration. All behind #ifdef __linux__.

Create src/platform/src/WindowsDriver.h/.cpp: WFP (Windows Filtering Platform) interface stubs, ETW (Event Tracing for Windows) interface stubs, Windows Service registration stubs. Behind #ifdef _WIN32.
Create src/platform/src/MacOSDriver.h/.cpp: EndpointSecurity framework interface stubs, Network Extension interface stubs. Behind #ifdef __APPLE__.
Create src/platform/src/PlatformFactory.cpp: returns appropriate driver based on compile-time OS detection.
Update root CMakeLists.txt: add add_subdirectory(src/platform) (unconditionally), include(Platform) cmake module.
Ensure entire build still compiles with ENABLE_QT=OFF and all existing tests pass.
acceptance_criteria: cmake -B build -DENABLE_QT=OFF && cmake --build build succeeds without errors
cd build && ctest --output-on-failure passes all existing tests
src/platform/ module compiles as part of the build
IPlatformDriver.h defines a clean abstract interface
LinuxDriver compiles on Linux with inotify and /proc implementations
main.cpp has a complete Qt initialization path in #ifdef ENABLE_QT_BUILD
findings: All implemented successfully. The platform module compiles with LinuxDriver on Linux. Result<void, Error> uses ::ok() (no args) and ::err(Error). The Error class is defined in core/Error.h (not a separate Result.h). The build with ENABLE_QT=OFF produces the threatone_platform static library and all 1053 tests pass.
